title: A5 chat.phase.* spans drop on failure path (composing + analyzing-image)

correctness.notes: try/finally + outcome ladder strictly mirrors TTS pattern. Composing: outcome flips to 'error' in catch BEFORE mapOrchestratorError throws, finally emits span unconditionally with hasImage + outcome. Analyzing-image: outcome flips, re-throw preserves stack, finally emits with sessionId + outcome. Both sites capture startedAtMs BEFORE the await — durationMs is positive in failure path (time-to-failure visible). ChatPhaseOutcome imported as type-only from chat-phase-timer (single source of truth, no duplication).

scope_fidelity.notes: A5 R2/R3 strictly respected: analyzing-image span wraps `processImage` ONLY — `runOcrGuard` stays outside the finally block (line 225). Comment explicitly calls this out ('runOcrGuard stays OUTSIDE the wrap — span scope is strictly processImage per A5 R2/R3'). No drift into LLM-internal phases or sibling spans. Composing wrap is tight on `orchestrator.generate` only — `tryLlmCacheStore` and `commitResponse` remain outside. Out-of-scope items correctly deferred (classifyError + Prom error counter for these phases — finding scope was Langfuse drop only).

kiss.notes: Pattern replication, not abstraction — KISS-aligned. ~15 lines added per site, no extracted helper (good: 2 sites do not warrant abstraction yet). One minor cost: `Awaited<ReturnType<ImageProcessingService['processImage']>>` indirection on the `processed` variable declaration (acceptable — avoids importing the private `ProcessedImage` return type from an adjacent service and keeps the existing public surface unchanged). Mock-first test strategy (`jest.mock('@shared/observability/chat-phase-span', ...)` with `__getRecordedCalls` helpers) is pragmatic and lets assertions inspect the metadata payload — labels alone would miss `outcome`.

a11y.notes: N/A — backend observability fix, no UI surface.

security_honesty.notes: No new PII surfaces: composing metadata = { sessionId, requestId, hasImage, outcome }; analyzing-image metadata = { sessionId, outcome }. No textLength, no raw image bytes, no user content. Fail-open posture preserved via `safeTrace` inside `emitChatPhaseSpan`. `throw err` in analyzing-image catch preserves stack; `mapOrchestratorError(err, requestId)` re-thrown in composing catch preserves AppError subclasses (UPSTREAM_UNAVAILABLE 503, CircuitOpenError, etc.). UFR-013 honesty: comments cite exact line ranges of TTS reference (`text-to-speech.openai.ts:115-132`) — verified in source.

evidence: let outcome: ChatPhaseOutcome = 'success' declared before try
outcome = 'error' flipped in catch BEFORE re-throw via mapOrchestratorError
finally block emits emitChatPhaseSpan('composing', composingStartedAtMs, { sessionId, requestId, hasImage, outcome })
site_1_composing.delta_vs_baseline: +8 / -4 lines, comment block (4 lines) cites merged_bug_004 + TTS reference

evidence: let outcome: ChatPhaseOutcome = 'success' declared before try
processed declared via Awaited<ReturnType<ImageProcessingService['processImage']>> to avoid importing private return type
outcome = 'error'; throw err sequence in catch (re-throw preserves stack)
finally block emits emitChatPhaseSpan('analyzing-image', startedAtMs, { sessionId, outcome })
runOcrGuard remains OUTSIDE the try/finally (line 225, after the closing brace) — A5 R2/R3 scope respected
site_2_analyzing_image.delta_vs_baseline: +14 / -3 lines, comment block calls out runOcrGuard exclusion explicitly

tradeoffs_evaluated.tradeoff: No classifyError + Prom counter for composing / analyzing-image phases
tradeoffs_evaluated.rationale: Out of scope for merged_bug_004 (finding was Langfuse drop only). chatPhaseErrorsTotal Prom dimension is owned by ChatPhaseTimer for {stt,llm,tts} only — extending to {analyzing-image,composing,...} would expand the phase label set and break the existing Prom contract. Track as separate observability follow-up if engineers need error-class alerting on these phases.

ProcessedImage is a private structural type inside ImageProcessingService. Promoting it to a public domain type just to declare `let processed: ProcessedImage` would expand the port surface for purely local use. The Awaited indirection is a standard TypeScript idiom, fully resolved at compile time, and keeps the hexagonal boundary tight.

tradeoffs_evaluated.tradeoff: Mock `emitChatPhaseSpan` directly vs `safeTrace` label capture (a la existing A5 tests)
tradeoffs_evaluated.rationale: safeTrace labels do not surface the metadata payload — outcome verification requires inspecting the metadata object. Direct mock of @shared/observability/chat-phase-span with __getRecordedCalls helpers gives the test precise access to (phase, startedAtMs, metadata) triples. Pattern is isolated to this one test file; existing A5 span tests via safeTrace remain valid for their label-only assertions.

lint.outcome: PASS (eslint src/ + lint:test-discipline + tsc --noEmit all clean)

tests_targeted.details: Test Suites: 2 passed (existing chat-phase-spans + new chat-phase-spans-failure), Tests: 12 passed (6 existing + 6 new) — exact match to expected 12/12. ELIFECYCLE artifact is global coverage threshold (88% global) not met when running scoped tests; not a real failure.

tests_chat_scope.details: Test Suites: 169 passed, 6 skipped. Tests: 2131 passed, 49 skipped, 0 failed. Zero regression on chat scope.

tests_frontend.details: Test Suites: 249 passed. Tests: 2704 passed (unchanged from baseline). Snapshots: 4 passed.

red_tests_coverage_check.composing_error_outcome: COVERED (test 2 — orchestrator.generate rejects with AppError 503 UPSTREAM_UNAVAILABLE)
red_tests_coverage_check.analyzing_image_error_outcome: COVERED (test 5 — stripExif rejects with AppError 400 IMAGE_DECODE_FAILED, propagates out of processImage)
red_tests_coverage_check.duration_ms_positive_smoke: COVERED (test 3 composing both outcomes + test 6 analyzing-image both outcomes — startedAtMs typeof number, > 0, <= Date.now())
red_tests_coverage_check.metadata_payload_integrity: COVERED (every test asserts metadata.outcome via the spanMock.__getRecordedCalls() helper, not just label presence)
red_tests_coverage_check.verdict: All 6 expected red cases present and aligned to finding rubric.

top_findings.title: ChatPhaseOutcome type reuse from chat-phase-timer
top_findings.description: Both sites correctly import ChatPhaseOutcome as a type-only import from @shared/observability/chat-phase-timer — single source of truth. No type duplication risk.

top_findings.title: runOcrGuard scope guard documented inline
top_findings.description: prepare-message.pipeline.ts line 213 comment explicitly states 'runOcrGuard stays OUTSIDE the wrap — span scope is strictly processImage per A5 R2/R3'. Excellent guardrail for future drift.

top_findings.title: TTS pattern reference cited at exact line range
top_findings.description: Both comments cite text-to-speech.openai.ts:115-132. Verified in source: matches the synthesizing-voice try/finally + outcome block. Future readers can cross-reference.

top_findings.title: Optional follow-up: classifyError for composing/analyzing-image
top_findings.description: Out of scope for this finding, but as a future iteration the chatPhaseErrorsTotal Prom dimension could be extended to {analyzing-image,composing} with a stable error_type taxonomy (timeout / upstream_5xx / image_decode_failed / etc.). Track separately if alerting need surfaces.

recommendation.rationale: Fix is minimal, mirrors a proven pattern from the same A5 PR (synthesizing-voice TTS), introduces no regressions across backend (2131 chat-scope tests pass) or frontend (2704 unchanged), all 12/12 targeted phase-span tests pass (6 existing + 6 new), lint clean. Comments are honest and verifiable (UFR-013 compliant). Tradeoffs are correctly scoped (no over-engineering with Prom error counters out of finding scope). Weighted mean 92 / 100 — clears the 85 APPROVED bar.
follow_ups: Optional: extend chatPhaseErrorsTotal taxonomy to non-LLM phases in a separate observability ticket (not blocking).

Consider adding a lightweight ESLint custom rule or doc note that any new emitChatPhaseSpan call site MUST be inside a try/finally with an outcome attribute — would prevent future regressions of this exact class.
